AstroBis
Home Solar System Exoplanets ISS Tracker Small Bodies Star Atlas About About Author GitHub

Interactive astronomy platform - by Biswajit Jana @2026

AstroBis

A browser-native space lab for realistic Solar System scale, live orbital tracking, future asteroid approaches, interstellar visitors, exoplanet discovery data, and a dimensional stellar atlas.

Open Solar System Atlas Browse Exoplanets
01 Solar System Oort-scale Planets, dwarf planets, AU ruler, heliopause, and spherical Oort Cloud shells. 02 Exoplanets 4,000 rows NASA Archive snapshot with source links, mission plots, and distribution charts. 03 Small Bodies Through 2050 JPL close-approach data plus hyperbolic interstellar visitor context. 04 ISS Orbit Live Earth Textured 3D Earth, orbit projection, telemetry, and ground track.

Reference scale

The Universe by the Numbers

AstroBis keeps the large-scale science readable while preserving the sense of distance.

🌍
0+
Exoplanets Confirmed
Worlds orbiting other stars — and counting
🌌
0B
Galaxies Observable
In the observable universe alone
⏳
0M
Years — Universe Age
Years since the Big Bang
☀️
0
Planets in our System
Each unique — from tiny Mercury to vast Jupiter

Mission modules

What You Can Explore

From the Oort Cloud to live orbital traffic, AstroBis keeps the useful science close to the surface.

AU Three.js + WebGL

Solar System Atlas

A compressed real-distance Solar System with planets, dwarf planets, moon counts, asteroid belt, trojans, Kuiper belt, heliopause, AU ruler, and Oort Cloud shells.

Open Atlas ->
EXO NASA Archive

Exoplanet Explorer

NASA Exoplanet Archive data with measured radius, mass, orbit, temperature, discovery method, facility counts, distribution plots, and source references.

Browse Planets ->
ISS Live Orbit

ISS Mission Control

Track the station over a textured 3D Earth with live telemetry, orbit projection, cloud layer, and ground-track map.

Track ISS ->
NEO JPL CAD

Small-Body Watch

Look ahead through 2050 using JPL close-approach data with distance filters, speed, size estimates, risk-proxy sorting, and interstellar visitor orbits.

Open Watch ->
HR Star Data

3D Stellar Atlas

Navigate bright stars using real RA/Dec coordinates, spectral colors, constellation guides, distance shells, and an H-R diagram.

Open Atlas ->
APOD Daily NASA

NASA APOD

Each day, NASA selects an astronomy image with scientific context. AstroBis keeps it alongside the live data tools.

See Today ->

NASA APOD

Today's View from NASA

The Astronomy Picture of the Day, curated by NASA scientists.

🔭
Loading today's cosmos…

Begin at 1 AU

Start with the Solar System

Open the AstroBis atlas, travel from Mercury to the Oort Cloud, then jump into exoplanets, live ISS telemetry, future asteroid approaches, and the stellar map.

Open the Solar System Atlas
